headline: Consulting Giants Race to Own the AI Playbook—but the Real Game is Just Starting

stories.title: OpenAI's 'Frontier Alliances' Bet the Farm on Consultants for Enterprise AI Sales
stories.summary: OpenAI just dropped multi-year partnerships with BCG, McKinsey, and Accenture to push its enterprise tools into customer tech stacks. Basically OpenAI's saying: consultants are how we actually get enterprises to care about AI.
stories.takeaway: Consulting firms are becoming AI's distribution network—and they're getting access to cutting-edge tools to make it happen fr

stories.title: PE Firms Already Replacing $500K McKinsey Reports with $50K AI Agents—and the Results Are... Messy
stories.summary: DiligenceSquared raised $5M and convinced mega PE firms managing $2T+ to swap traditional consulting for AI-powered due diligence. Lowkey terrifying because they're stress-testing this on live multi-hundred-million-dollar deals.
stories.takeaway: AI consulting is cannibalizing traditional consulting's pricing power—but nobody knows if the AI actually works at scale yet

stories.title: Deloitte Drops Enterprise AI Navigator—Shifting AI from Cost Center to Revenue Driver
stories.summary: Deloitte just launched a platform to help companies move AI from "expensive experiment" to "actually making money." Includes AI opportunity scanners and agent creation hubs—basically democratizing what the Big 3 used to gatekeep.
stories.takeaway: The consulting playbook is getting productized and the pyramid model is crumbling—jr consultants' jobs just became way more AI-dependent

watchNext.title: How AI Is Reshaping Consulting's Core Economics—And Why Strategy Firms Are Freaking Out
watchNext.why: Tbh this breaks down why McKinsey & co are losing ground to execution-heavy firms like Accenture—and why the entire consulting value proposition is getting flipped upside down

watchNext.title: 2026 CEO Outlook: 61% of Leaders Can't Hire AI Talent Fast Enough—Consulting Firms Are About to Profit Massive
watchNext.why: KPMG's latest CEO survey shows the skills gap is widening and consulting is basically the only place companies can turn—this is where the real AI consulting revenue gets made
